From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Football league season

[Namibia Premiership](/wiki/Namibia_Premiership "Namibia Premiership")

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Season | 2024–25 |
| Dates | November 2024 – June 2025 |
| Champions | [African Stars](/wiki/African_Stars_F.C. "African Stars F.C.") |
| ← [2023–24](/wiki/2023%E2%80%9324_Namibia_Premiership "2023–24 Namibia Premiership")  *[2025–26](/w/index.php?title=2025%E2%80%9326_Namibia_Premiership&action=edit&redlink=1 "2025–26 Namibia Premiership (page does not exist)")* → | |

The **2024–25 Namibia Premiership** was the third season of the [Namibia Premiership](/wiki/Namibia_Premiership "Namibia Premiership"), the top-tier [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") league in [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia"). [African Stars](/wiki/African_Stars_F.C. "African Stars F.C.") were the reigning champions after winning the championship for the second consecutive season,[[1]](#cite_note-Stars_Back_to_Back_Champions-1) and retained their title.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The new clubs promoted for the season were: [KK Palace](/wiki/KK_Palace "KK Palace") from the Northwest Division, [Cuca Tops FC](/wiki/Cuca_Tops_FC "Cuca Tops FC") from the Northeast Division, and [Namibia Correctional Service FC](/wiki/Namibia_Correctional_Service_FC "Namibia Correctional Service FC") from the Southern Division. Cuca Tops FC returned to the nation's top flight for the first time in twenty-two years, last appearing in the [Namibia Premier League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_League "Namibia Premier League") in the 2002–03 season.[[3]](#cite_note-Cuca_Tops_Secure_Promotion-3)[[4]](#cite_note-KK_Palace,_NCS_promoted-4)

[Orlando Pirates](/wiki/Orlando_Pirates_S.C. "Orlando Pirates S.C.") did not participate in the top-flight for the first time after being relegated the previous season. [Life Fighters](/wiki/Life_Fighters_F.C. "Life Fighters F.C.") and [Okakarara Young Warriors](/w/index.php?title=Okakarara_Young_Warriors&action=edit&redlink=1 "Okakarara Young Warriors (page does not exist)") were also relegated.[[1]](#cite_note-Stars_Back_to_Back_Champions-1)
